raw_consumer_statement:
I visited family in the XXXX area from XX/XX/XXXX to XX/XX/year>. Subsequently, between XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/year>, a person unknown to me used my credit card to make several purchases in XXXX TX and XXXX TX. I originally reported the dispute on XX/XX/year> after checking my account via the Citi app. CitiBank determined the physical card was present at the time of the purchases and rejected/denied my claim for credit protection from the disputed charges. I was not in Texas at the time the purchases were made. I did not authorize any person the use of the credit card. There are no other authorized users on the credit card account. 

XX/XX/year> XXXX XXXX XXXX # XXXX {$20.00} XXXX XXXX # XXXX, XXXX  TX {$200.00} XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX  TX {$180.00} XX/XX/year> XXXX XXXX XXXX TX {$46.00} XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX TX {$13.00} XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX TX {$36.00} XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX TX {$230.00} XXXX XXXX, XXXX  TX {$87.00} XX/XX/year>XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX TX {$100.00} On XX/XX/year> I again contacted CitiBank to dispute the fraudulent charges. On XX/XX/year>, credit protection for four ( 4 ) of the fraudulent charges was granted. XXXX XXXX XXXX TX {$46.00}, XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX TX {$13.00}, XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX TX {$36.00}, and XXXX XXXX  XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX  TX {$20.00}. 

I have since received two ( 2 ) letters from CitiBank, dated XX/XX/year>, claiming that I remain liable for the remaining disputed, unresolved fraudulent charges.
